name: Common First Year Engineering
description: Foundation courses common to all B.Tech branches across all AICTE-approved engineering programs.

subjects.name: Engineering Mathematics I
subjects.description: Single variable calculus, differential equations, matrix algebra, and vector calculus forming the mathematical foundation for all engineering disciplines.
learning_outcomes: Solve first and second order differential equations
Apply matrix operations and compute eigenvalues
Evaluate limits, derivatives, and integrals
Analyze sequences, series, and convergence
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational for all engineering analysis, simulation, and optimization work

subjects.name: Engineering Mathematics II
subjects.description: Multivariable calculus, Fourier series, Laplace transforms, complex analysis, and numerical methods for engineering applications.
learning_outcomes: Compute partial derivatives and multiple integrals
Apply Laplace and Fourier transforms to solve ODEs
Use numerical methods for root-finding and integration
Analyze functions using complex analysis techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for signal processing, control systems, and simulation engineering

subjects.name: Engineering Physics
subjects.description: Wave optics, quantum mechanics, semiconductor physics, electromagnetism, and modern physics with engineering applications.
learning_outcomes: Apply wave optics principles to interference and diffraction
Explain quantum mechanics concepts and their engineering relevance
Analyze semiconductor behavior and device characteristics
Solve electromagnetic field problems using Maxwell's equations
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for electronics, photonics, materials science, and nanotechnology

subjects.name: Engineering Chemistry
subjects.description: Chemical thermodynamics, electrochemistry, corrosion science, water treatment, polymer chemistry, and spectroscopic techniques for engineering.
learning_outcomes: Apply thermodynamic principles to chemical processes
Analyze electrochemical cells and corrosion mechanisms
Evaluate water quality and treatment methods
Identify polymer types and their engineering applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for chemical, materials, environmental, and process engineering

subjects.name: Programming for Problem Solving
subjects.description: Introduction to computational thinking, algorithm design, and programming in C. Covers variables, control flow, functions, arrays, pointers, and file I/O.
learning_outcomes: Design algorithms for computational problems
Write, compile, and debug C programs
Use pointers, arrays, and structures effectively
Implement file I/O operations in C
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for all software development roles across engineering domains

subjects.name: Basic Electrical Engineering
subjects.description: Fundamentals of DC and AC circuits, electrical machines, transformers, and power systems. Covers network theorems, phasor analysis, and electrical safety.
learning_outcomes: Analyze DC and AC circuits using network theorems
Determine performance characteristics of transformers
Explain working principles of DC and AC machines
Apply electrical safety practices and grounding techniques
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for power, manufacturing, automotive, and infrastructure sectors

subjects.name: Engineering Graphics
subjects.description: Orthographic projections, isometric drawings, sectioning, development of surfaces, and computer-aided drafting using CAD software.
learning_outcomes: Create orthographic and isometric projections of engineering objects
Prepare sectional views and development of surfaces
Use CAD software for 2D and 3D modeling
Interpret engineering drawings and blueprints
subjects.industry_relevance: Universal engineering skill for design, manufacturing, and construction industries

subjects.name: Workshop Practice
subjects.description: Hands-on training in fitting, carpentry, welding, sheet metal, smithy, foundry, and basic electrical wiring. Emphasizes workshop safety and measurement tools.
learning_outcomes: Perform basic fitting, carpentry, and welding operations
Fabricate sheet metal components using hand tools
Demonstrate safe workshop practices and tool handling
Measure and inspect components using precision instruments
subjects.industry_relevance: Hands-on foundation for manufacturing, production, and maintenance roles

subjects.name: English Communication
subjects.description: Development of professional communication skills including technical writing, presentation skills, group discussions, interviews, and inter-personal communication.
learning_outcomes: Write clear technical documents, reports, and emails
Deliver effective presentations with visual aids
Participate in group discussions and interviews confidently
Demonstrate professional etiquettes in workplace communication
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential professional skill for every engineering graduate across all sectors

subjects.name: Environmental Science
subjects.description: Ecosystems, biodiversity, natural resources, environmental pollution, climate change, sustainable development, and environmental legislation.
learning_outcomes: Analyze ecosystem structure, function, and biodiversity
Evaluate environmental impact of engineering projects
Identify pollution sources and control methods
Apply principles of sustainable development
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory for environmental compliance in all engineering industries

subjects.name: Constitution of India
subjects.description: Historical background, preamble, fundamental rights and duties, directive principles, federal structure, parliamentary system, judiciary, and constitutional amendments.
learning_outcomes: Explain the historical evolution of the Indian Constitution
Identify fundamental rights, duties, and directive principles
Describe the structure of union and state governments
Analyze the role of judiciary and constitutional amendments
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory for engineering graduates under AICTE and UGC guidelines

subjects.name: Universal Human Values
subjects.description: Self-exploration, harmony in human relationships, family, society, and nature. Understanding value education, ethical decision-making, and professional ethics.
learning_outcomes: Articulate personal and professional values for engineering practice
Analyze ethical dilemmas using value-based frameworks
Demonstrate harmony in relationships and society
Integrate human values into engineering decision-making
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for ethical engineering practice and responsible innovation

subjects.name: Physics Lab
subjects.description: Experimental verification of physics principles including optics, electricity, magnetism, and modern physics using laboratory instruments and data analysis.
learning_outcomes: Use laboratory instruments such as spectrometers and oscilloscopes
Verify physical laws through systematic experimentation
Analyze experimental data and estimate measurement errors
Prepare scientific lab reports following standard formats
subjects.industry_relevance: Hands-on skills for R&D, quality testing, and instrumentation roles

subjects.name: Chemistry Lab
subjects.description: Hands-on experiments in volumetric analysis, pH measurement, conductivity, chromatography, and synthesis of compounds with emphasis on lab safety.
learning_outcomes: Perform volumetric and gravimetric chemical analyses
Operate pH meters, conductometers, and spectrophotometers
Synthesize and characterize chemical compounds
Maintain lab safety protocols and chemical waste disposal
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for chemical, pharmaceutical, materials, and environmental testing labs

subjects.name: Programming Lab
subjects.description: Hands-on programming exercises in C covering algorithms, data structures, debugging, and problem-solving with weekly lab assignments and mini-projects.
learning_outcomes: Write and debug C programs for algorithmic problems
Implement data structures including arrays, linked lists, and trees
Use debugging tools and version control for code management
Complete mini-projects demonstrating programming proficiency
subjects.industry_relevance: Direct preparation for software development internships and entry-level roles

subjects.name: Workshop Lab
subjects.description: Practical workshop sessions in fitting, carpentry, welding, sheet metal, and foundry with focus on precision measurement and safety procedures.
learning_outcomes: Fabricate components using fitting, carpentry, and welding
Operate drilling, grinding, and sheet metal machines safely
Measure finished components for dimensional accuracy
Demonstrate workshop organization and safety compliance
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for production and manufacturing industry roles

subjects.name: Engineering Graphics Lab
subjects.description: Computer-aided drafting using AutoCAD or equivalent software for creating 2D drawings, 3D models, assemblies, and rendered visualizations.
learning_outcomes: Create 2D engineering drawings using CAD software
Build 3D solid models and parametric assemblies
Generate orthographic and isometric views from 3D models
Apply dimensions, tolerances, and annotations to drawings
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential CAD skills for mechanical, civil, aerospace, and automotive design
